# portainer-stacks

> GitOps-managed Docker Compose stacks for a self-hosted Portainer CE instance on macOS. Each stack is a standalone service managed via Portainer's GitOps polling. Secrets are never stored in git — they are set in Portainer's Environment Variables UI per stack.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): Setup guide, new developer checklist, GitOps wiring instructions
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Coding and contribution conventions

## Stacks

- [gluetun](stacks/gluetun/README.md): WireGuard VPN gateway (Surfshark). Required by deluge, jackett, qbittorrent.
- [qbittorrent](stacks/qbittorrent/README.md): Torrent client. Routes through gluetun.
- [deluge](stacks/deluge/docker-compose.yml): Torrent client (alternative). Routes through gluetun.
- [jackett](stacks/jackett/docker-compose.yml): Torrent indexer proxy. Routes through gluetun.
- [sonarr](stacks/sonarr/docker-compose.yml): TV show download automation.
- [n8n](stacks/n8n/README.md): Workflow automation, backed by Postgres 16.
- [watchtower](stacks/watchtower/docker-compose.yml): Nightly image auto-updates for all running containers.

## Key conventions

- Stack folder name must match the Portainer stack name exactly
- All images use `:latest` — Watchtower handles updates
- Volume paths use `DOCKER_DATA_HOME` and `DOCKER_SHARED_HOME` env vars set in Portainer UI
- GitOps uses polling (24h interval) — webhooks do not work with a LAN-hosted Portainer instance
- gluetun must start before deluge, jackett, and qbittorrent
